_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to apply Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry to solve three-dimensional problems.

_source.w: We are learning to apply Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry to solve three-dimensional problems.
y: Extending Pythagoras and trigonometry to 3D develops our ability to work with spatial problems that have real-world applications.
3D problems require identifying appropriate right triangles within more complex figures — a challenging but rewarding spatial reasoning skill.
These skills are used in architecture, engineering, aviation, mining and geographic information systems.
eg: Finding the length of the space diagonal of a rectangular prism using Pythagoras' theorem twice.
Calculating the angle of elevation from a point on the ground to the top of a building, accounting for horizontal distance.
Finding the angle between a slant edge and the base of a square pyramid.
Solving: 'A ladder leans against a wall at an angle, and the wall is not vertical. Find the true height reached.'
Calculating the shortest distance across the surface of a cylinder between two points.
Using trigonometry to find the angle of depression from a lookout to a boat at sea.
